page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2i-c1m4.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, utilizing Intel Xeon processors running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The system is configured with 1 thread per core, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation without hyperthreading.

This server is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include any local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning storage requirements must be met via external network-attached block storage. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 3 Gbps, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given its 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance offers a stable resource profile for general-purpose workloads. The lack of hyperthreading provides predictable compute performance, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for applications requiring consistent CPU execution without the overhead of shared-thread contention. It is suitable for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and application hosting.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2i-c1m4.xlarge is a general-purpose, x86_64 architecture server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores operating at 2.7 GHz, with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration that ensures dedicated CPU allocation. The instance features 16.0 GB of RAM, translating to 4.0 GB of memory per core, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external block storage for data persistence. With its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated compute resources, this server is designed for predictable performance in general-purpose applications, web serving, and moderate database hosting where hyperthreading overhead is undesirable.
